name: Valle del Ebro — circular desde Pancorbo — Parque Natural de Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil
region_intro: Mountain biking around Pancorbo offers a diverse landscape characterized by river valleys, natural parks, and historical sites. The region features significant elevation changes, with routes traversing through areas like the Valle del Ebro and the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park. Riders can expect varied terrain, including challenging climbs and descents through gorges and along reservoirs. The area provides a range of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Pancorbo The most popular mountain bike trail is Valle del Ebro — circular desde Pancorbo — Parque Natural de Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil, a 36.0 miles (57.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult circular route explores the Valle del Ebro and the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Trail along the GR 99 – Presa de Sobrón Road loop from Embalse de Sobrón, a difficult 15.2 miles (24.5 km) path. This route follows sections of the GR 99 trail near the Sobrón Reservoir, offering varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Presa de Sobrón Road – Trail along the GR 99 loop from Bomberos, a 14.3 miles (23.0 km) trail leading through the area around Presa de Sobrón, often completed in about 2 hours 16 minutes. Mountain biking around Pancorbo is defined by river valleys, natural park landscapes, and routes near reservoirs. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels, with significant elevation gains. The routes in Pancorbo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 150 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Pancorbo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for mountain bike trails around Pancorbo?
A: Mountain bike trails in Pancorbo are primarily categorized as moderate to difficult. There are 14 moderate routes and 10 difficult routes available, offering challenges for experienced riders. There are currently no easy routes listed.

Q: What kind of distances can I expect on mountain bike routes around Pancorbo?
A: Routes vary significantly in length. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100327938/valle-del-ebro-circular-desde-pancorbo-parque-natural-de-montes-obarenes-san-zadornil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Ebro — circular desde Pancorbo — Parque Natural de Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 58 km, while the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10514539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pancorbo Gorge loop from Miraveche&lt;/a&gt; is around 33 km.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike trails in Pancorbo?
A: Yes, many routes around Pancorbo are circular. A notable example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100327938/valle-del-ebro-circular-desde-pancorbo-parque-natural-de-montes-obarenes-san-zadornil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Ebro — circular desde Pancorbo — Parque Natural de Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the Valle del Ebro and Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10514539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pancorbo Gorge loop from Miraveche&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What options are available for advanced mountain bikers in Pancorbo?
A: Pancorbo offers 10 difficult mountain bike routes, providing significant challenges. These often feature substantial elevation gains and varied terrain, including sections through gorges and along reservoirs. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38191630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the GR 99 – Presa de Sobrón Road loop from Embalse de Sobrón&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1,100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Pancorbo?
A: The mountain bike trails around Pancorbo are generally rated as moderate to difficult, with no easy routes currently listed. Riders should have some experience and be prepared for varied terrain and elevation changes. Families with younger or less experienced riders might find the routes challenging.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails around Pancorbo?
A: The trails often traverse scenic areas like the Valle del Ebro and the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park, offering views of river valleys and gorges. You can also encounter historical sites and natural landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31895160" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Marta Castle – Church of St. James loop from Pancorbo&lt;/a&gt; passes by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4111346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Marta Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4111336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of St. James&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Pancorbo?
A: The best times for mountain biking in Pancorbo are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable riding through the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or notable landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several points of interest. You might encounter historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4111346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Marta Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4111336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of St. James&lt;/a&gt;. There are also natural viewpoints, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3872925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Watchtower&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes, including the Pancorbo Gorge.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Pancorbo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic river valleys, and the challenging routes that explore natural parks like Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil. The diverse landscape with significant elevation changes is a common highlight.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Pancorbo?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails available around Pancorbo, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels, predominantly moderate to difficult.

Q: Where do mountain bike trails typically start in Pancorbo, and is parking available?
A: Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100327938/valle-del-ebro-circular-desde-pancorbo-parque-natural-de-montes-obarenes-san-zadornil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Ebro — circular desde Pancorbo — Parque Natural de Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil&lt;/a&gt;, start directly from Pancorbo itself. For routes beginning outside the main town, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38191630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the GR 99 – Presa de Sobrón Road loop from Embalse de Sobrón&lt;/a&gt;, you can often find parking near the designated starting points or nearby villages like Embalse de Sobrón or Miraveche.
tips: Imposing castle a few steps from the town of Pancorbo. Even if it was in an open field, it is worth seeing, but climbing the stairs to the tower, admiring the town below and feeling yourself under the cliffs of the upper mountains... an experience to be repeated.
I do not understand this viewpoint, being almost a stone's throw away, that of Peña Mazo, which has better views of the gorge. If anything, this one is right next to the parking lot.
An authentic architectural and religious treasure of Gothic style and Renaissance elements. The church, located on top of a small hill, captivates with its beauty and splendor.

Medieval-style village with stone houses and cobbled streets, surrounded by beautiful natural landscapes. The town was walled and remains of the old wall can still be seen. The oldest documentary references date back to the beginning of the 11th century.

The top of Otero Mayor is 789 meters above sea level. The climb is not too difficult and the reward is worth it. Thanks to its elevated situation, it offers an incomparable panoramic view of mountains, valleys and nature.
